title: Robust Adversarial Weighted Meta Reinforcement Learning for Generalizable Traffic Signal Control
authors: Kaiwen Zhang, Zhiqun Hu, Yueting Wang, Zhaoming Lu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Deep reinforcement learning (DRL)-based traffic signal control (TSC) algorithms often suffer from overfitting to static training environments and perform poorly in unseen traffic scenarios. Two major challenges remain in previous methods: the scarcity and homogeneity of training data, and the limited adaptability to harsh or challenging traffic environments. To address these issues, we propose RAW-MetaRL, a Robust Adversarial Weighted Meta-Reinforcement Learning framework. RAW-MetaRL introduces an adversarial environment agent that adaptively generates increasingly challenging and diverse traffic environments based on the current policy’s performance, where synthetic environments are optimized to expose weaknesses in the current control policy. Furthermore, we design a weighted meta-learning framework that alternates between local-level adaptation on individual tasks and global-level adaptation across a sampled set of tasks, aiming to effectively train the meta-agent. A meta weight generator is incorporated to prioritize rare and critical environments, enabling the meta agent to generalize effectively across diverse and previously unseen environments. Extensive experiments on real-world and synthetic datasets demonstrate that RAW-MetaRL significantly outperforms existing methods in adaptability and performance across diverse traffic environments.

title: MFTFormer: Meteorological-Frequency-Temporal Transformer with Block-Aligned Fusion for Traffic Flow Prediction
authors: Qiannan Shen, Jing Zhang
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: Urban traffic flow prediction is critical for intelligent transportation systems, yet remains challenging due to complex spatiotemporal dependencies and external weather influences. Existing methods suffer from three key limitations: (1) uniform time-domain modeling fails to disentangle periodic trends from aperiodic fluctuations, (2) weather information is integrated through simple concatenation without considering multi-granularity impacts, and (3) lack of interpretability hinders understanding of weather-traffic interactions. To address these issues, we propose MFTFormer, a novel framework that synergistically combines time-frequency decomposition with hierarchical weather-aware fusion. Our approach introduces three key innovations: (1) a dual-domain encoder with mixture-of-experts that separately processes trend and seasonal components via specialized frequency and temporal attention networks, (2) a block-aligned fusion module that hierarchically reorganizes features through convolutional refinement and weather-conditioned modulation, capturing both immediate and cumulative meteorological effects, and (3) interpretable attention mechanisms that reveal dynamic spatial dependencies under varying weather conditions. Extensive experiments on four real-world datasets (PeMS04, PeMS08, Metro-Traffic, METR-LA) demonstrate that MFTFormer achieves state-of-the-art performance with an average 6.2% MAE reduction over strong baselines, while providing actionable insights into weather-traffic relationships through attention visualization.

title: Frequency-Domain Adaptive Mixing: A Novel Transformer Architecture for Non-Stationary Time Series
authors: Jialin Guo
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: Time series forecasting with non-stationary data remains challenging due to temporal distribution shifts and complex multi-scale dynamics. While recent Transformer-based methods have achieved remarkable progress, they often struggle with non-stationarity and fail to fully exploit frequency-domain information. In this paper, we propose FDAM (FrequencyDomain Adaptive Mixing), a novel Transformer architecture that addresses these limitations through three key innovations. First, Frequency-Domain Adaptive Normalization performs adaptive normalization on magnitude and phase components separately in the frequency domain to handle non-stationarity. Second, Spectral Adaptive Mixing learns frequency-dependent channel correlations to capture varying inter-variable relationships across different frequency bands. Third, Learnable Frequency Gate automatically selects task-relevant frequency components through data-driven gating. Extensive experiments on six benchmark datasets demonstrate that FDAM achieves state-of-the-art performance, with average improvements of 3.8% in MSE and 2.9% in MAE over existing methods.

title: Analysis of abnormal transaction map and risk identification algorithm in data asset trading platform
authors: Zewu Peng, Yingwei Liang, XinyaoFeng
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: With the rapid development of data factor market, the non-standardization and high correlation of data asset transactions bring challenges to the traditional risk control system. In this paper, a multi-modal dynamic graph neural network (MDGNN) is proposed to identify abnormal transactions and quantify risk propagation in the data asset trading platform. Firstly, a four-dimensional heterogeneous map of user-data-capital-behavior is constructed, and the dynamic evolution of trading network is described by sliding window mechanism. Secondly, a Heterogeneous Graph Attention Network (HGAT) is designed to adaptively capture the importance differences of neighboring nodes, integrating multi-modal features such as transaction temporal patterns and data asset sensitivity labels; Then through Monte Carlo simulation to quantify the risk propagation path, a risk assessment system including 12 indicators such as transaction entropy and capital concentration is constructed. The experiment based on $\mathbf{8 6 0, 0 0 0}$ real transaction data of a large data asset trading platform shows that the MDGNN model F1score reaches 0.709, and the AUC-ROC is 0.923, which is 15.7% higher than the existing optimal method, and the average processing time is only 2.5 minutes, which can effectively identify the typical risk patterns such as money laundering transaction ring and illegal reselling of sensitive data, and provide an interpretable technical governance tool for regulators.

title: A Consortium Blockchain Scheme for Secure Collaboration in BIM
authors: Sihan Li, Min Wang, Guoxin Huang, Yuna Chen
publish: 2026 International Conference on Blockchain Technology and Foundation Models (BTFM)
abstract: Blockchain technology, with its traceability and tamper-proof features, provides a viable solution for defining responsibilities and enabling traceability in Building Information Modeling (BIM) design processes. To address the limitations of existing BIM collaboration platforms in terms of compatibility, storage efficiency, and traceability convenience, we propose a secure BIM collaboration scheme based on enterprise consortium blockchain. The scheme treats a whole BIM model file as the fundamental on-chain unit, a single file is stored for each block instead of incremental data storage. Therefore, it is compatible with multiple BIM model formats, significantly reducing the number of generated blocks, and decreasing on-chain latency. Based on the scheme, we design and implement a BIM collaboration and traceability platform to ensure reliable data collaboration while maintaining compatibility and usability. Our scheme boosts blockchain storage and traceability efficiency in BIM Collaboration by 85.7%, while provide reference for BIM information in intelligent construction. It also demonstrates a concrete engineering case where blockchain empowers trustworthy computing and decentralized data sharing.

title: A Practice Report Plagiarism Detection System Based on Simhash Algorithm
authors: Zhenhui Wang
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: In order to solve the problem of control and formative assessment of practical teaching in the transformation of higher education towards ability cultivation, reduce the phenomenon of plagiarism in practical reports, and improve the quality of teaching process assessment, a practical report plagiarism detection system based on Simhash algorithm is designed and developed. The system uses the SSM framework to build the backend and Vue technology to implement front-end development. After processing the text through the IKAnalyzer tokenizer, the Simhash algorithm is used to generate document signatures, and the Hamming distance calculation is combined to obtain plagiarism detection results. This system can efficiently complete the repetition detection of practical reports, help teachers objectively evaluate the completion of homework, reduce teaching burden, strengthen process control, and effectively curb plagiarism behavior, help create a good learning atmosphere, and provide technical support for improving the quality of practical teaching.

title: Adversarial Dual Teacher Based Teacher-Student Transformer for Cross-Domain Object Detection
authors: Xing Wei, Cang Liu, Chong Zhao, Fan Yang, Yang Lu, Benhong Zhang
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: Domain Adaptive Object Detection (DAOD) aims to enhance object detection in a target domain by transferring knowledge from the labeled source domain to the unlabeled target domain. Recently, the teacher-student framework has proven effective in domain-adaptive object detection tasks, where the teacher model generates target domain pseudo-labels to guide the student model’s learning. However, this framework is prone to accumulating errors from the teacher model, which can ultimately degrade the performance of the student model. In this paper, we propose an Adversarial Dual Teacher Based Teacher-Student framework (ADT). Specifically, we introduce an auxiliary teacherstudent framework with a weight-swapping strategy, building on which we combine the pseudo-labels from the auxiliary teacher and the original teacher to generate high-quality pseudolabels. Additionally, we design a multi-level adversarial feature alignment to further enhance the domain-adaptive capability of the student model. Experiments conducted on three DAOD benchmark datasets demonstrate that ADT is adaptable and effective.

title: Ontology Matching with Heterogeneous Graph Neural Network
authors: Daoqu Geng, Shuai Zhang
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: Ontology matching supports data integration and interoperability by aligning semantically corresponding concepts, properties, and relations across ontologies. Many existing approaches rely primarily on node-level semantic information and underutilize contextual structural cues in the ontology graph, which can limit matching accuracy. To address this issue, we propose a relation-aware heterogeneous graph neural network framework. Our method first fine-tune BERT to obtain node representations, then extract relation representations, and construct a heterogeneous graph. Next,iteratively updating and fusing node and relation embeddings to strengthen their mutual interactions. Finally, computing a cross-ontology similarity matrix using cosine similarity between node embeddings and apply the Hungarian algorithm to derive an optimal one-to-one alignment. Evaluating the proposed method on three tasks from the OAEI Bio-ML dataset, and the results show that it achieves substantially higher F1 scores than existing methods on two of the tasks.

title: Adoption of Generative AI Policies in Computing Education: A Longitudinal Syllabus Analysis
authors: Ngoc Thanh Bui, Aijuan Dong
publish: 2026 International Conference on Semantic Computing (ICSC)
abstract: Educational institutions lack a systematic understanding of how generative AI policies are implemented across computing courses, and keyword-based methods alone do not capture their nuance and complexity. This study presents a syllabus-based framework for analyzing generative AI policy adoption practices and trends in computing education. Policyrelated text is identified through keyword-based retrieval with contextual expansion and classified into policy types using a combination of rule-based inference and embedding-based semantic similarity. The resulting policy categories (No Policy, Permitted, Restricted, Discouraged, and Prohibited), together with extracted course metadata, enable analysis of adoption patterns over time and across course levels. Using 148 course syllabi from Spring 2024 to Fall 2025, results show that adoption increased from $\mathbf{12.8 \%}$ to $\mathbf{58.8 \%}$ over four semesters, with similar adoption rates between undergraduate $(40.4 \%)$ and graduate $(39.6 \%)$ courses. Instructor concerns are identified via semantic matching, with primary emphasis on over-dependence $(72.9 \%)$, proper citation $(57.6 \%)$, and appropriate use $(52.5 \%)$. This work provides a validated methodology ($90.56 \%$ accuracy) for characterizing generative AI policy adoption in higher education.

title: Design and Multi-variable Coordination Optimization of MPC Controller for Air-flow Silk-drying Machine
authors: Haoyu Lan, Jiamin Lei, Jianfeng Chen, Zhenye Chen, Qi Zhang, Long Zhao, Xitong Wang, Haowei Chen
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: To address the limitations of traditional manual and PID control in airflow-type tobacco drying processes–including significant moisture fluctuations at the outlet, challenges in coordinating multi-variable coupling, and slow response with large hysteresis-we developed a hierarchical optimization intelligent control system. The solution employs model predictive control (MPC) as the core framework, integrating feedforward-feedback collaborative mechanisms to establish a hybrid dynamic model that accurately captures the nonlinear and hysteresis characteristics of the drying process. Through extended state-space modeling, the system combines mechanism prior knowledge with data-driven learning, while introducing rolling-time-domain optimization strategies for real-time parameter adjustments. Experiments covering steady-state performance, dynamic response, and disturbance resistance demonstrated that: 1) The MPC system stabilized the outlet moisture standard deviation below 0.12, achieving a 21% improvement over traditional PID; 2) Under a 0.5% moisture step disturbance at the inlet, the system reduced recovery time to 4 minutes and decreased overshoot by 67%. Key innovations include: ①Dual-mode MPC architecture enabling multi-variable decoupling; ②Adaptive weight adjustment mechanism enhancing operational adaptability; ③Hybrid modeling technology balancing model accuracy and computational efficiency. This research significantly improves the stability and automation level of tobacco drying processes, providing a replicable technical paradigm for intelligent upgrades in tobacco and other drying industries.

title: Implementation of the MFCC-Streaming Bi-LSTM Based Speech Emotion Recognition Method
authors: Bo Zhu, Lu Cao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: We propose a lightweight MFCC-Streaming Bi-LSTM baseline for speech emotion recognition. Five-second windows are first extracted and converted to 180-dimensional MFCC vectors by a streaming encoder; a two-layer bidirectional LSTM then performs sequence modelling without any multi-modal cues, data augmentation or heavy hyper-parameter tuning. On the combined CREMAD, SAVEE and RAVDESS datasets including 12,699 utterances, the system achieves 82.86 % test accuracy and 71.9 % seven-class macro-recall, peaking at 86.0% on individual corpora. Inverse-frequency weighted random sampling raises the F1 of the minority “surprise” class from 48% to 63%, markedly mitigating long-tail bias. The framework offers an interpretable, reproducible and low-latency reference for emotion recognition in resource-constrained scenarios.

title: Unsupervised Boilerplate Removal Using N-gram Sequence Analysis
authors: Kishore Vanapalli, Shahzad Khan, M. Omair Shafiq
publish: 2026 International Conference on Semantic Computing (ICSC)
abstract: Boilerplate content in news articles, including advertisements, trending sections, and recommendation modules, poses challenges to data cleaning, data extraction and downstream NLP applications within knowledge engineering pipelines. In this paper, building on the related work, we propose an unsupervised data engineering technique that uses n-gram patterns to effectively identify and eliminate boilerplate HTML tags and class-based placeholders commonly inserted by publishers. By analyzing news articles with identical or near-identical content from wire services and syndicates, our approach intelligently detects boilerplate content and identifies superfluous tags and classes specific to each publisher domain. This enables accurate knowledge extraction and content curation from HTML pages at runtime, supporting data quality objectives essential for knowledge engineering systems. Empirical evaluations demonstrate robust performance across a diverse corpus of news websites, underscoring the technique’s adaptability and accuracy in handling heterogeneous data sources. This approach enables data preprocessing pipelines to provide a lightweight yet powerful boilerplate removal mechanism that enhances data quality and enables reliable downstream NLP-based analytics and semantic processing.

title: YOLOv8-based Robotic Dynamic Target Tracking Algorithm
authors: YuLong Wang, Ling Liao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: Target tracking is one of the core capabilities for robots to achieve autonomous perception, environmental interaction, and task execution, and its performance directly determines the application value of robots in complex scenarios. The YOLO (You Only Look Once) series algorithms have become the mainstream solution in the field of target detection due to their balanced advantages in real-time performance and accuracy. Among them, YOLO8, as the latest iterative version, has achieved comprehensive improvements in detection speed, accuracy, and generalization ability, providing new technical support for the optimization of robot target tracking technology. This paper focuses on YOLO8-based robot target tracking technology, systematically elaborates on the core characteristics and technical advantages of the YOLO8 algorithm, constructs a YOLO8-based robot target tracking system architecture, and analyzes the adaptation scheme and application effect of technology implementation in combination with the industrial inspection scenario. Experimental verification shows that the YOLO8-based robot target tracking system can achieve a tracking accuracy of 92.3% in complex environments with an average tracking delay of less than 30 ms, demonstrating excellent real-time performance and robustness.

title: Adaptive Dynamic Live-Line Work Bypass Load-Bearing Platform Leveling Kit
authors: Changlin Leng, Mingjie Luo, Zhuqin Luo, Haitao Wang, Wu Pu, Yi Zhang, Erjie Lu, Shanshan Li, Biao Tang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: With the continuous increase in requirements for power system operational reliability and supply continuity, liveline bypass construction has become increasingly widespread in transmission and distribution maintenance. During bypass operations, the load-bearing platform is susceptible to factors such as changes in conductor tension, shifting operational loads, and external environmental disturbances under complex working conditions. These factors can lead to platform attitude imbalance, adversely affecting operational safety and construction efficiency. Addressing the limitations of existing bypass platform leveling methods-specifically their inadequate adaptability and restricted dynamic response capabilities-this paper proposes an adaptive dynamic leveling system for live-line bypass platforms. By integrating attitude sensing, dynamic leveling actuators, and adaptive control units, the system enables real-time monitoring and rapid adjustment of platform posture changes, maintaining stability and horizontal alignment across diverse operational conditions. Structural design analysis, operational principle evaluation, and field application verification demonstrate that this integrated system outperforms conventional leveling solutions in precision, response speed, and operational stability. It significantly enhances the safety and reliability of live-line bypass construction. These findings provide valuable insights for advancing the intelligent and integrated development of live-line work equipment.

title: Modeling and Optimization of Particle Beam of Linear Accelerator Machines Using AI for Health Application
authors: Zairov Temirlan, Ilyas Muhammad, Abdullah Aamir Hayat, Shawana Noor
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: This work investigates the application of artificial intelligence for improving patient lateral positioning accuracy in external beam radiotherapy using a linear accelerator. A predictive setup optimization framework based on a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) neural network is proposed to compensate for patient motion, particularly respiratoryinduced displacement. The system is evaluated using a programmable QA phantom tool and a motorized treatment couch, with parameters calibrated from real measurements acquired on a Varian TrueBeam STX linear accelerator. Simulation results demonstrate a median post-correction residual positioning error of 0.6 mm, with 95% of cases remaining below the 2 mm clinical tolerance threshold. The results demonstrate that the proposed AI-based prediction framework can improve patient positioning accuracy and may support real-time adaptive radiotherapy workflows.

title: Integrating Hand Interaction and AI Guidance for Mixed Reality Passthrough
authors: Ajune Wanis Ismail, Fazliaty Edora Fadzli, Amalia Syazana Binti Mohd Mazlimin
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: The development of immersive technology, such as Augmented Reality (AR), Mixed Reality (MR), and Virtual Reality (VR), has greatly impacted the teaching-learning process in the education system. These technologies utilize the learning experience, encouraging the learners to actively participate in the learning experience and have been used in various chemistry lab activities, among other subjects. However, the available learning applications have mainly incorporated the use of AR and VR, with little emphasis on the use of MR passthrough, which allows the real environment to be captured by the camera of the headset. Moreover, there remains a notable gap in the incorporation of hand interaction and Artificial Intelligence (AI) guidance to enrich the learning process. To address this gap, this paper presents the implementation of an MR passthrough chemistry laboratory experiment that integrates AI-driven guidance and hand interaction to provide a more effective and immersive learning experience.

title: Interconnected Observer-Based SMC for DC–DC Converters With Disturbances and Noises
authors: Peng Liu, Zuo Wang, Jinya Su, Ji Wang, Shihua Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: The sliding mode controller (SMC) is inherently sensitive to noise due to its discontinuous control, especially under mismatched disturbances. To improve the noise robustness and disturbance rejection of SMC, this work proposes a tightly integrated framework for DC–DC converters. Rather than simple combinations of separate modules, it seamlessly unifies a filter, disturbance observer (DOB), and SMC. A novel discrete-time interconnected observer, fusing a DOB and an adaptive Kalman filter, simultaneously reconstructs disturbances and suppresses noise. By leveraging filtered states and disturbance estimates, compensation for both mismatched disturbance and input discretization dynamics is explicitly embedded into an improved sliding mode surface. Rigorous stability analysis of the closed-loop system is provided, and comparative simulations and experiments on DC–DC converter are conducted against three benchmark control methods.

title: MetaGen Blended RAG: Unlocking Zero-Shot Precision for Specialized Domain Question-Answering
authors: Kunal Sawarkar, Shivam Raj Solanki, Abhilasha Mangal
publish: 2026 International Conference on Semantic Computing (ICSC)
abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) struggles with domain-specific enterprise datasets, often isolated behind firewalls and rich in complex, specialized terminology unseen by LLMs during pre-training. Semantic variability across domains like medicine, networking, or law hampers RAG’s context precision, while fine-tuning solutions are costly, slow, and lack generalization as new data emerges. Achieving zero-shot precision with retrievers without fine tuning still remains a key challenge. We introduce ‘MetaGen Blended RAG’, a novel enterprise search approach that enhances semantic retrievers through a metadata generation pipeline and hybrid query indexes using dense and sparse vectors. By leveraging key concepts, topics, and acronyms, our method creates metadata-enriched semantic indexes and boosted hybrid queries, delivering robust, scalable performance without fine-tuning. On biomedical PubMedQA dataset, MetaGen Blended RAG achieves 82% retrieval accuracy and 77% RAG accuracy, surpassing all prior zero-shot RAG benchmarks and even rivaling fine-tuned models on that dataset, while also excelling on datasets like SQuAD and NQ. This approach redefines enterprise search using new approach to building semantic retrievers with unmatched generalization across specialized domains.

title: Bridging Hardware and Semantics: An LLM-Enhanced Ontology Framework for Explainable Anomaly Detection in IoMT Systems
authors: Thrity Golzari, Zhangying He, Hossein Sayadi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Semantic Computing (ICSC)
abstract: This paper presents an LLM-enhanced ontology framework for cybersecurity in Internet of Medical Things (IoMT) environments, integrating hardware performance counter (HPC) features, device metadata, and known vulnerabilities to enable reasoning-driven, explainable anomaly detection. The study introduces a hybrid human-LLM ontology development workflow that accelerates ontology construction and refinement while preserving conceptual rigor and semantic consistency. It further investigates how large language models (LLMs) can augment ontology engineering through a comparative evaluation of three state-of-the-art models: ChatGPT-o3, ChatGPT-4o, and Gemini 2.5 Pro, assessed across structural and semantic dimensions. Using the proposed LLM-enhanced ontology, we achieve 84.29% anomaly (e.g., malware) classification accuracy from HPC data while mapping detected anomalies to relevant vulnerabilities and device properties. Experimental results demonstrate that LLMs significantly improve ontology quality and reasoning coherence, with Gemini 2.5 Pro achieving the best balance between expansion and consistency.

title: Study of Early Weak Fault Injection of Broken Rotor Bar in Traction Motor
authors: Li Liu, Junfei Nie, Wu Shao, Jintian Yin, Xinbo Liu, Zhaoying Zeng
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: Analyze the early weak faults of the rotor broken bars in high-speed train traction motors, and the relationship between fault damage factor and phase resistance is obtained; the simulation platform of traction drive system is constructed, and the simulation injection of early weak fault of traction motor is realized by the method based on model through the fault injection module, using the relationship between fault damage factor and phase resistance change. Experimental results demonstrate the rationality and effectiveness of the injection method.

title: Construction of a Task-Driven Model for BIM Course Cost Training Based on OBE Concept
authors: Zhenzhen Geng, Xiaojiang Du
publish: 2026 International Conference on Blockchain Technology and Foundation Models (BTFM)
abstract: Traditional Building Information Modeling cost estimation training often suffers from fragmented task design, weak linkage between skills and knowledge, and unbalanced assessment mechanisms, resulting in inefficient learning and insufficient competency formation. To address these problems, this study develops a task-driven training model grounded in the Outcome-Based Education philosophy. The model integrates task decomposition, knowledge–skill mapping, and closed-loop feedback, constructing a dynamic and data-driven learning process that continuously aligns performance indicators with instructional goals. Using data from 56 students in a comprehensive BIM cost estimation course, experimental results show significant improvements: task completion rate increased by 10.0%, knowledge mastery by 12.4%, feedback response time reduced by 42.6%, and learning engagement enhanced by 18.1% compared with traditional teaching. These findings verify the model’s effectiveness in strengthening precision, collaboration, and adaptability. The study offers a replicable framework for intelligent, measurable, and outcome-oriented BIM education within engineering training.

title: A Precise Prediction Method for Competitive Athletes’ Specialized Performance Based on MultiDimensional Training Data Fusion
authors: Yang Yu
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: With the rapid advancement of sports science and intelligent training technologies, precise prediction of athletes’ specialized sports performance has become essential for scientific training optimization, injury prevention, and competitive strategy formulation. However, existing performance prediction methods are often limited by single data sources, inefficient data fusion, and insufficient prediction accuracy. To address these limitations, this paper proposes a precise prediction method for athletes’ specialized sports performance based on multi-dimensional training data fusion. First, multi-dimensional training dataincluding physiological indicators, kinematic parameters, training load, and historical performance records-are collected and preprocessed to reduce noise and redundancy. Then, an improved attention-based multi-modal fusion module is designed to effectively integrate heterogeneous data while emphasizing key performance-related features. Finally, a hybrid prediction framework combining Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks and Gradient Boosting Decision Trees (GBDT) is constructed to capture both temporal dependencies and nonlinear feature relationships. Experiments conducted on real-world training data from track and field sprinters demonstrate that the proposed method significantly outperforms traditional singlemodel approaches (LSTM, GBDT, and Support Vector Machine). Specifically, the mean absolute error (MAE) is reduced by 12.3% 27.8%, and the root mean square error (RMSE) is reduced by $\mathbf{1 0. 7 \% - 2 5. 9 \%}$, with improved prediction stability. The proposed method provides an effective technical solution for precise performance prediction and promotes the development of intelligent sports training systems.

title: EM³R: An Explainable Modular Multi-LLM Reasoning Framework for Question Answering System
authors: Siwen Zhao, Sen Lin, Wei Wang, Yujun Li
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: Entailment tree generation provides a structured paradigm for explainable question answering by explicitly modeling multi-step logical inference. However, most existing large language model (LLM)-based approaches rely on a single model and treat reasoning as an unconstrained generation process, resulting in limited controllability over intermediate inference steps. As a result, early reasoning errors readily propagate across steps, while large search spaces induced by distractor facts hinder reliable entailment tree construction. To address these challenges, we propose the Explainable Modular Multi-LLM Reasoning framework (EM<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>R), which reframes entailment tree construction as an interactive, verifier-guided reasoning process. EM<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>R follows a stepwise Select–Decompose–Propose–Verify workflow that explicitly separates evidence selection, goal planning, step generation, and entailment validation. Specifically, a Semantic Selector filters hypothesis-relevant facts, a Goal Decomposer derives intermediate subgoals, a Step Proposer generates executable entailment steps, and an Entailment Verifier validates each step before it is incorporated into the proof. Verified steps are then assembled into a structured and human-interpretable entailment tree. Experiments on the EntailmentBank benchmark show that EM<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>R produces more coherent and structurally consistent entailment trees than strong baselines. Under the strict AllCorrect evaluation, EM<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>R achieves a 44.1% improvement in overall accuracy, indicating substantially improved global reasoning correctness. These results demonstrate that explicitly controlling search and validating intermediate reasoning steps is critical for reliable and interpretable multi-step question answering.

title: A Fake News Detection Based on Cross-Social Media Communication Characteristics
authors: Jiaxu Wu, Yuan Gao, Ke Ji
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: To address existing fake news detection limitations-overreliance on single-platform offline datasets, ignorance of cross-platform expression variations, and insufficient consideration of dissemination characteristics-this paper proposes DFAS (Cross-social media Dissemination Feature-based Fake News Detection Model), a dissemination feature-driven model. It integrates news content and dissemination information: web crawling collects multi-source data (reports, timelines, comments) from diverse platforms; eight benchmark neural networks generate content feature vectors, while statistical methods extract propagation features; the two are fused with postheuristic error correction. Experiments confirm DFAS significantly improves detection accuracy and efficiency.

title: Integrating Neural Machine Translation and Large Language Models for Multilingual Semantic Search
authors: Pavol Batalik, Roman Kosik, Jakub Dubec, Jan Lang
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: This paper presents a multilingual semantic search system that integrates neural machine translation (NMT) with large language models (LLMs) to enable efficient, languageagnostic document retrieval. The proposed approach leverages Meta’s M2M-100 translation model alongside advanced embedding generators—BGE, E5, and Sentence-BERT—to unify multilingual inputs within a dense vector space. A custom synthetic dataset of multilingual queries and summaries, generated using Google’s Gemini API, was used to evaluate retrieval accuracy and resource efficiency across English, German, and Slovak. Experimental results show that while BGE and E5 achieve high recall (up to 0.98), Sentence-BERT consistently attains the highest precision across languages. Comparative analyses of GPU, CPU, and memory utilization reveal practical trade-offs between embedding quality and computational cost. The findings highlight that combining neural translation with LLM-based embeddings enables robust cross-lingual semantic retrieval, offering a scalable framework for multilingual digital libraries and information systems.

title: Research on Dynamic Execution Optimization of Smart Contracts Based on GRPO Algorithm
authors: Ruixue Xu, Sijia Lian, Suhua Liu, Yuanjie Zhu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Blockchain Technology and Foundation Models (BTFM)
abstract: Aiming at the core pain points such as low execution efficiency, high resource consumption, and insufficient dynamic adaptability caused by the "deploy-and-freeze" characteristic of traditional blockchain smart contracts, this paper proposes a dynamic execution optimization scheme for smart contracts based on the Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) algorithm [1]. Specifically, the "group sampling + relative advantage" mechanism, which is the core of the GRPO algorithm, is implemented through two key modules: for the group sampling module, the algorithm first divides the smart contract execution state space into multiple sub-scenarios based on key feature dimensions such as transaction type, data volume, and network congestion degree, then randomly selects 3–5 candidate execution actions from each sub-scenario and forms a candidate action group by fusing actions from different sub-scenarios; for the relative advantage calculation module, instead of adopting the absolute advantage evaluation method of the traditional Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) algorithm [2], it introduces a relative advantage function that takes the average execution effect of the candidate action group as the reference benchmark, quantifies the advantage of each candidate action relative to other actions in the group through indicators such as Gas cost saving rate, execution delay reduction rate, and task completion rate, and weights the relative advantage values to determine the optimal execution action. With the GRPO reinforcement learning algorithm as the core driving force, the scheme constructs a three-layer collaborative architecture consisting of an off-chain Artificial Intelligence (AI) decision-making layer, an oracle data layer, and an on-chain contract execution layer. Through real-time state perception, group sampling action generation, and scenario-based reward function design, it realizes the dynamic adaptive adjustment of smart contract execution strategies. Experimental results show that in typical application scenarios such as Decentralized Finance (DeFi) lending and supply chain finance, compared with traditional static contracts and optimization schemes based on the mainstream PPO algorithm, this scheme can reduce the average Gas fee by 22%~25%, lower the non-performing loan rate from 3.2% to 1.1%, and control the execution response delay within 500ms, significantly improving the execution efficiency, resource utilization, and dynamic adaptation capability of smart contracts. This research provides a new technical path for solving the problem of dynamic execution of smart contracts and has important theoretical and practical significance for promoting the efficient and trusted operation of the Web3 ecosystem.

title: Finite Element Analysis of Microstrip Feeding Antenna and its Parametric Management
authors: Qianyue Nie
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: For the microstrip feeding antenna, finite element analysis of the original model and associated structures are conducted, resulting in some numerical results such as electric field intensity, S-parameter, 3D and 2D radiation patterns. Then, a parametric management App is constructed based on the results of finite element analysis, by changing the structural parameters and (or) working parameters of the microstrip feeding antenna, the solid model, grid model, nephograms of electric field intensity, S-parameter and radiation patterns can be obtained in real time.

title: Research on Safety Management and Control of Power Operation Sites Based on YOLOv Algorithm
authors: Zhen Niu, Chengzhang Wang, Li Min Li, Zhonghua Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: In power operation sites, only directly acquired on-site images can be used, resulting in low accuracy of safety management and control. Therefore, a safety management and control method for power operation sites based on the YOLOv algorithm is proposed. To address the problem of frequent interference with on-site images, a preprocessing method based on multi-scale fusion and adaptive enhancement is proposed to improve image quality. The YOLOv algorithm is introduced to accurately extract dangerous features from aspects such as cluster center selection and feature dimension processing. A safety management and control system is constructed, using the soft-max function to evaluate the probability of dangerous features, defining information entropy to quantify safety risks, formulating management and control strategies by optimizing model parameters at the cloud and edge, and using vector cross product to determine illegal intrusion behavior, thus achieving safety management and control of power operation sites. Experimental results show that the initialization of the 3-layer convolutional layer has a relatively high detection frame rate at various iteration counts, reaching 47 frames per second after 600 iterations, which can promptly warn of potential hazards. In safety management and control tests, the design method has significant advantages in indoor and outdoor scenarios, accurately capturing dangerous behaviors. Regarding the false alarm rate of violations, with 500 samples, the false alarm rate of the method in references [1]–[4] was 31 %-36 %, while that of the design method was only 19%, with an increase of 8-9 percentage points less than other methods. It can more comprehensively and accurately identify violations and has excellent performance and strong practicality in the safety management of power operation sites.

title: Research on Lifetime Prediction Method for Electrochemical Energy Storage Systems Based on Multi-model Fusion
authors: Weiwei Li, Huijuan Huo, Cheng Xin, Tianqiong Chen, Shuo Wang, Jing Duan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: With the advancement of energy structure transformation and dual carbon goals, electrochemical energy storage systems play a crucial role in renewable energy integration and grid operation. Accurate remaining useful life (RUL) prediction is vital for maintenance strategy formulation and risk reduction. However, existing single-model approaches suffer from limited generalization and robustness. This paper proposes a multi-model fusion-based lifetime prediction method for electrochemical energy storage systems. It constructs a heterogeneous model pool with BP neural network, SVM, and LSTM, and develops a two-layer ensemble strategy of “performance threshold + weighted fusion + linear stacking”, excluding ELM to avoid instability. Experimental results show that the Weighted Fusion Model achieves the lowest RMSE (128.8 cycles) and highest $\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}} \boldsymbol{(} \mathbf{0. 7 9 8 8} \boldsymbol{)}$, outperforming single models. This method enhances adaptability to complex degradation patterns and robustness under uncertainties, providing theoretical and technical support for precise RUL prediction. Future work will integrate advanced algorithms and physical models, and extend to multi-scenario applications.

title: Dual-Branch RGB-TIR Network for PV Module Fault Detection: Training on UAV Imagery from Multiple Sites
authors: Tolulope Jayeola, Ilyas Muhammad, Michal Kačmařík
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: Dual-branch RGB-thermal infrared (TIR) deep networks are increasingly being utilized for Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV)-based photovoltaic (PV) module fault detection, but most published evidence remains single-site and does not rigorously demonstrate cross-installation robustness under varying panel types, layouts, and environmental conditions. To address this, this paper presents a two-phase research pathway toward a robust multi-site PV fault detection using a dual-branch RGB-TIR framework analyzed across heterogeneous datasets. The phase 1 of this study comprises of assessments conducted on RGB and TIR datasets from public repositories to characterize model behavior, training dynamics, and dataset-driven limitations before deployment on proprietary, higher quality self-acquired site imagery. This study identifies practical data-centric requirements for stable training which involves allocating approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4 0 - 6 0 \%}$</tex> background/null imagery to reduce false positives and mitigate overfitting towards defect-only images. This enforces distinct and sufficiently represented defect classes while excluding rare or visually confusable categories, and finally, adopting oriented bounding boxes (YOLOv8-OBB) for RGB imagery to accommodate the rotated geometry of PV modules and defect extents in Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) imagery. Phase 2 of this study will transfer these insights into the assessment of our self-acquired imagery, with the goal of improving cross-site generalization and operational readiness for scalable PV operations and maintenance.

title: Development of Novel Neural Network Architecture for Automated Pharmaceutical and Vitamin Identification Using Image Classification
authors: Ilyas Rashitov, Ualikhan Sadyk, Baglan Tolebay
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: Automated identification of pharmaceutical drugs and vitamins is critical for improving medication safety and reducing human errors in healthcare systems. This study proposes a novel multi-scale, attention-enhanced convolutional neural network designed for fine-grained classification of pharmaceutical images. The model integrates multi-scale feature extraction to capture both local visual details (e.g., imprints, edges) and global characteristics (e.g., shape, color distribution), while an attention mechanism emphasizes discriminative regions, enabling accurate differentiation of visually similar classes. Transfer learning with ImageNet-pretrained weights accelerates convergence and improves generalization. The model was evaluated on the Pharmaceutical Drugs and Vitamins Synthetic Images dataset, comprising 100,000 images across ten classes. Experimental results demonstrate high classification performance, achieving a macro-averaged F1-score of 0.897, with per-class F1-scores ranging from 0.766 to 0.934. ROC analysis and confusion matrices confirm robust discriminative capability, although challenges remain for classes with minimal visual differences. Compared to conventional CNN architectures, the proposed approach shows improved precision, recall, and F1-scores. The study contributes a scalable, accurate framework for automated pharmaceutical identification, offering a foundation for integrating synthetic data augmentation, multimodal features, and few-shot learning in future research.

title: Cross-scale Color Rendering Mechanism and High Fidelity Reproduction Model of Color 3D Printing
authors: Wei Yu, Chunwei Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: This article focuses on the cross-scale color rendering and high-fidelity reproduction problems of color 3D printing. In this article, by deeply analyzing the basic theory of cross-scale color rendering in color 3D printing, the influence of material characteristics, nozzle accuracy and model structure on color rendering is clarified, a high-fidelity reproduction model based on this mechanism is constructed, and color correction, optimization and cross-scale color consistency algorithms are designed. Photosensitive resin, nylon, PLA (polylactic acid) and other materials were selected to print model samples with different complexity. The results show that the color correction algorithm can effectively reduce the color deviation of the red channel of the standard color card, and the corrected data points are more concentrated on the ideal value. After several generations of evolution, the fitness value of the optimization algorithm increased significantly, and better color parameters were found. Cross-scale color consistency algorithm ensures the smooth change of cross-scale color parameters of complex model samples. The comprehensive assessment shows that the high-fidelity reproduction model and algorithm significantly improve the similarity between the actual color and the design color. The research proves that the model and algorithm can effectively solve the problems related to color 3D printing, and provide strong support for improving printing quality.

title: Research on Automatic Target Localization Method of Train Hook Picking Robot Based on Binocular Vision
authors: Zhirong Zhao, Dalong Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: Hook-picking robots often use monocular vision system for target localization, which needs to rely on pre-precisely calibrated parameters to convert two-dimensional image coordinates into three-dimensional spatial coordinates, resulting in low localization efficiency, so study the automatic target localization method of train hook picking robot based on binocular vision. The robot uses binocular vision system to determine the hook area synchronously. In the image of the hook area, the points with significant changes in gray scale are defined as feature points, and the feature points are extracted by Gaussian filtering, and the feature point matching is completed by the Euclidean distance measure of similarity to generate the correct matching point set. Based on the successfully matched feature point pairs, the three-dimensional coordinates of the hitch are restored using the principle of stereo triangulation, and the depth, horizontal and vertical coordinates of the feature points are calculated to realize the relative three-dimensional localization. The experimental results show that the method has a total relative error of only 2.76% in terms of localization accuracy, which is significantly better than that of the comparison methods of $6.67 \%, 8.56 \%$, and 7.37%, while the average time consumed in terms of localization speed is only 0.8 to 1.1 s, which demonstrates an excellent real-time performance in target localization of train-hook picking robots.

title: LLM-based Text-to-SQL: A Case Study of Geospatial Information Retrieval
authors: Temitayo Olamilekan Ogunsusi, Jose Angel Nguema Azeme Mangue, Weishan Bai, Xinyue Ye, Lijun Qian, Xishuang Dong
publish: 2026 International Conference on Semantic Computing (ICSC)
abstract: Text-to-SQL aims to convert natural language questions into executable SQL queries for retrieving information from databases. Despite significant advancements, a key limitation in current research is the lack of focus on geospatial databases. This paper addresses that gap by exploring LLM-based Text-to-SQL for geospatial information retrieval. We first construct a new benchmark using a PostGIS spatial database, specifically designed for Text-to-SQL tasks in the geospatial domain. Specifically, we use LLMs to generate additional user-SQL query pairs, enhancing the benchmark’s size and diversity. We also provide a statistical analysis of its characteristics, including query length, SQL complexity, and difficulty levels. Afterwards, we implement a Text-to-SQL pipeline leveraging state-of-theart (SOTA) large language models, including GPT-4, Llama, and Google’s Gemma. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed pipeline effectively retrieves geospatial information from the spatial database based on natural language queries, as evaluated using Execution Accuracy (EX) and Valid Efficiency Score (VES).

title: Research on Perspective Transformation Correction Combined with YOLOv8’s Intelligent Arm Vibration Detection Technology
authors: Haoyu Lan, Yunting Liang, Jiamin Lei, Turui Ning, Ziyang Zeng, Huanchao Liu, Tianyu Liang, Yingxiang Wu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: Arm swing parameters serve as critical indicators for assessing mechanical equipment operation. Traditional detection methods relying on manual measurements or contact sensors suffer from low efficiency and significant measurement errors. This paper presents a computer vision-based non-contact detection strategy for robotic arms, which employs perspective transformation to eliminate image distortion and integrates the YOLOv8 deep learning model for intelligent positioning of vibration angular points. This approach enhances detection accuracy and automation levels, providing a reliable solution for industrial equipment condition monitoring.

title: Air Quality in Kazakhstan: A Review of Monitoring, Modeling, and Machine Learning Approaches
authors: Khaled Mohamad
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: This review studies the state of air quality research in Kazakhstan, focusing on key pollution sources, health and environmental impacts, monitoring techniques, and regulatory frameworks. By analyzing recent studies, we identify emerging trends, research gaps, and methodological limitations. Special attention is given to the role of machine learning (ML) in overcoming data scarcity, improving predictive accuracy, and enhancing real-time air quality monitoring. These results emphasize the critical necessity of enhancing sensor networks, implementing data-informed policy measures, and adopting interdisciplinary strategies to address pollution effectively.

title: StatFeat-WheatID: Statistical Image Feature-Based Identification of Wheat Varieties Using Machine Learning
authors: Sushil Kumar Verma, Vicky Khanduja, Utkarsh Dwivedi, Kush Parmar, Digvijay Singh Chauhan, Basant Kumar
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: Wheat identification is of prime importance in its supply chain, breeding, and quality management. Manual identification of items, in fact, remains difficult, unpredictable, and error-susceptible. In this research, there comes forward a new statistical imaging technique for wheat variety identification through machine learning-based digital image processing. More than 6800 labeled images have been used for creating a high-quality labeled dataset for six mainstream varieties of wheat: DBW-303, DBW-187, UAS-347, HD-2285, WH-1270, and HD-2967. The identification technique uses Digital Image Processing (DIP)-Machine Learning (ML) pipeline to trains traditional learners for statistical features such as area, perimeter, solidity, eccentricity, features of Gray Level Co-occurrence Matrix (GLCM) texture, and intensity features of RGB grain images. With 99.12 % accuracy and an F1-Score of 0.99, Support Vector Machine (SVM) outperformed Random Forest, which had 97.8% accuracy and an F1-Score of 0.97. The results indicate that effective machine learning algorithms, along with manually designed statistical variables, can accurately identify wheat types without relying on deep learning models, thus facilitating low-cost, real-time implementation.

title: Design of Torsion Bar Spring Based on Intelligent Optimization Algorithm and CAE Technology
authors: Jianbao Zhu, Luan Li, Zhongjie Fan, Chongyan Zhao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: To address the issues of high cost and time consumption in the design and development of torsion bar spring components, this study proposes a design method based on intelligent particle swarm optimization algorithm and CAE technology. Taking the torsion bar spring of a certain vehicle as an example, the optimal structural size parameters of the torsion bar spring are obtained through particle swarm optimization design, and the performance of the optimized torsion bar spring was simulated and validated using CAE technology. The results show that its performance meets the requirements and resulting in an improvement in its energy storage capacity. The proposed method enhances design efficiency and yields satisfactory outcomes, offering valuable insights for the design of automotive components.

title: Multimodal Emotion Recognition in Korean Speech Based on Pre-trained Models
authors: Lu Cao, Bo Zhu, Zhanbo Yang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: Speech emotion recognition is crucial for humancomputer interaction and affective computing, especially in language-specific scenarios like Korean. Existing methods often fail to fully exploit complementary acoustic and linguistic information, limiting performance on minority emotion categories. To address this, we propose a Korean multimodal emotion recognition framework integrating Wav2Vec2.0 as the acoustic backbone and KoBERT as the linguistic backbone, with a cross-modal attention fusion mechanism. The framework extracts utterance-level acoustic and textual embeddings via end-to-end fine-tuning, then dynamically weights modality contributions to capture cross-modal emotional correlations. Experiments on KEMDY-19 and AIHub datasets show the model achieves 73.3% test accuracy and $\mathbf{8 5. 5 2 \%}$ validation unweighted average recall, outperforming state-of-the-art baselines and confirming effectiveness for both majority and minority emotion categories.

authors: Zhixin Zhao, Yitao Hu, Simin Chen, Mingfang Ji, Wei Yang, Yuhao Zhang, Laiping Zhao, Wenxin Li, Xiulong Liu, Wenyu Qu, Hao Wang

title: Relation Extraction in Electronic Information Equipment Based on Bert and Neural Network*
authors: Mengwu Xue, Xiaofang Wu
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: In recent times, as the domains of artificial intelligence and electronic information equipment have seen swift advancement, the convergence of electronic information equipment with artificial intelligence is emerging as a future pivotal trend in the field of electronic information equipment. Relation extraction, a critical technique in knowledge extraction, is instrumental in distilling essential insights from the vast and sparse data within the electronic and electromagnetic domains. In this study, we have compiled a comprehensive data set comprising 300,000 words of detailed equipment and parameter data specific to the electronic information equipment domain. We have also developed a novel relation extraction model, Bert-TKNB, which takes advantage of the strengths of BERT’s embeddings and bidirectional encoding, the LSTM gating mechanisms, the KAN neural network, and a Naive Bayes classifier to enrich sentence semantics. The model conducted comparative experiments and ablation experiments on the public dataset SKE and the private electronic information dataset ELWF. The final results show that the proposed method exhibits better performance in relation extraction in comparison with the baseline models adopted in the experiment.

title: Human Injury Degree Identification Questionanswering System Based on Large Language Model and Knowledge Graph
authors: Zhuo Xing
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: Human injury degree often becomes a debate focus of cases in courts, which depends on the interpretation of expert witness heavily. Hence, a human injury degree identification question-answering system was presented based on Large Language Model (LLM) and Knowledge Graph (KG). The proposed system includes four core modules: knowledge graph, question rewriting, knowledge retrieval and answer generation. The results show that this question-answering system could answer related questions concerning human injury degree identification, which manifest good accuracy and high practicability.

title: Short-Term Wind Power Forecasting Using a Hybrid Attention-Based xLSTM-Transformer Framework
authors: Bowen Zhang, Jian Wang, Jingya Xu, Tingyu Dai
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: Wind power prediction can enhance the stability of the power system. Traditional prediction methods find it difficult to capture the long-term correlations and complexity in time-series data. Here, this paper presents a short-term wind power forecasting model based on an attention-enhanced extended Long Short-Term Memory (xLSTM)-Transformer architecture. The xLSTM module is introduced to extract long-term time-series features and improve memory performance, and then the Transformer structure is used to explore global relationships within the sequence and effectively represent complex wind power characteristics. Based on the experimental results, the proposed model has achieved stable convergence and high prediction accuracy. In addition, it can promptly respond to rapid changes and peak variations. Therefore, the proposed method can effectively address the problem of high-accuracy short-term wind power prediction and support the stable operation of renewable energy systems.

title: Overview of Elevator Door Machine Fault Diagnosis Methods
authors: Yuncai Shi, Luqi Jiao, Yongfa Xiao, Lei Guo, Xiaolei Yang, Zhenzhen Liu, Chen Zhang, Yipu Gao
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: As a critical component in elevator operation, the health status of the door operator system directly impacts elevator safety and operational efficiency. Currently, there is limited research literature on diagnosing faults or failures in elevator door operator systems, necessitating further studies on diagnosing common door operator malfunctions. This paper reviews the application of Extreme Learning Machines (ELM), Multiscale Convolutional Neural Networks (MSCNN), and Nonlinear Vector Autoregressive Convolutional Neural Networks (NVAR-CNN) in elevator door operator fault diagnosis. A multidimensional unified analysis framework is constructed to compare these three methods through a combination of quantitative and qualitative evaluations. Concurrently, this study addresses data-related challenges and model cross-scenario generalization issues in door operator fault diagnosis, proposing a strategy for combined method application. This provides a comprehensive and practical reference for subsequent elevator door operator fault diagnosis research.

title: Adaptive Segments Fusion with Context-Aware Gaussian Constraint for Partially Relevant Video Retrieval
authors: Zhaoxing Li, Xianfeng Chen, Dongqin Liu, Jiao Dai, Jizhong Han
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: Given a text query, partially relevant video retrieval (PRVR) aims to retrieve large-scale untrimmed videos containing query-relevant moments. Existing PRVR methods utilize fixed clips or a couple of frames for video segment modeling, which fails to adapt to the diverse temporal spans of different video moments, leading to suboptimal performance in capturing fine-grained temporal relationships. In this paper, we propose an Adaptive Segments Fusion with Context-Aware Gaussian Constraint for Partially Relevant Video Retrieval to address this limitation. We design a learnable Content-Aware Sigma Generator that predicts the temporal span of each frame’s corresponding segment based on frame and local context features, enabling adaptive fusion of segment information. Then we propose the Content-Aware Regularization Loss to boost multi-scale temporal modeling. Extensive experiments show the proposed method enhances the model’s ability to capture diverse temporal structures of video moments, and outperforms other PRVR methods, with improved retrieval accuracy.

title: Robust Federated Learning for Short Time-Series Sales Forecasting: A Privacy-Preserving Approach
authors: Wei Qiu
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: This paper addresses the critical trade-off between data privacy and predictive accuracy in short-term sales forecasting, particularly in data-scarce enterprise environments. We propose a robust Federated Learning (FL) framework integrating Gradient Boosting Decision Trees (GBDT) with an anomaly-resilient aggregation protocol. By utilizing SignSGD and Differential Privacy (DP), the framework ensures commercial confidentiality while overcoming data scarcity through collaborative intelligence. Experimental results demonstrate superior R2 performance and high resilience against adversarial data injection.

title: AI-Driven Intelligent Optimization of Envelope Line Types for Oscillation Data Prediction and Accuracy Verification
authors: Min Wu, Chengxiu Dong
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: Aiming at the demand for high-precision prediction of small-sample oscillation sequences, an AI-driven gray envelope band prediction model is proposed. Envelope lines are constructed by intelligently extracting the upper and lower extreme values of raw data, and the optimal selection between linear and curvilinear envelope lines is systematically explored. Accuracy verification is completed from three aspects: error analysis, model comparison, and industry standard adaptation. Taking the water level oscillation data from Xiaomeikou Station in Taihu Lake Basin as the research object, Origin7.0 is used for envelope line fitting, and MATLAB is employed to implement AI parameter optimization. The prediction results of the proposed model are compared with those of the traditional $\operatorname{GM}(1,1)$ model and BP neural network model. Experimental results show that the average relative error (ARPE) of the curvilinear envelope line prediction is only 1.19%, which is significantly superior to the linear envelope line (1.38%), BP neural network (2.56%), and traditional $\operatorname{GM}(1,1)$ model (3.19%). All prediction errors meet the requirements of industry standards ($\leq 5 \%$), and the curvilinear envelope line has stronger adaptability to extreme oscillation nodes. This study verifies the effectiveness of AI-driven envelope line type optimization, provides a high-precision and verifiable technical solution for small-sample oscillation sequence prediction, and enriches the application practice of AI in the field of data prediction.

title: Edge-Deployed Conversational LLM Agents for Semantic Service Matching
authors: Jonas Scheffler, Guido Dartmann, Alexander Koß, Stephan Multhaupt, Lejla Begic Fazlic
publish: 2026 International Conference on Semantic Computing (ICSC)
abstract: Rural communities often face limited access to digital services due to infrastructural constraints and lower levels of digital literacy. Conventional form-based platforms require precise inputs and predefined categories, creating barriers for users unfamiliar with structured digital systems. This work presents a conversational platform that leverages a Large Language Model (LLM) agent to connect citizens with local companies through natural dialogue. The system integrates a conversational agent with tool-calling capabilities, a retrieval pipeline over a curated company database, and lightweight backend and web components. To ensure accessibility and sustainability, it employs open-source, quantized LLMs deployable on edge hardware. Evaluation across 260 representative user queries shows that compact 4-bit models, such as Qwen3 8B, achieve high decision accuracy in tool invocation as well as parameter extraction accuracy while maintaining feasible decoding speed and energy efficiency on an NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano Super. These findings demonstrate that small open-source LLMs can support inclusive, low-power conversational services, reducing reliance on centralized infrastructure and advancing digital inclusion in rural contexts.

title: PolAlpha-Net: A Lithology Segmentation Network Guided by Polarimetric Decomposition Knowledge for Dual Polarization SAR Images in Vegetated Areas
authors: Liming He, Zenghui Qin, Yongliang Tang, Jiuyang Cai, Chen Xue, Yu Liu, Zhixiang Liu
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: The classification of lithology through remote sensing technology has become increasingly vital for geological surveys and resource exploration. However, accurately classifying lithology in areas covered by vegetation remains a significant challenge. In this paper, we leverage the robust penetration and coherence capabilities of ALOS-1 dual-polarization SAR data to propose a new deep learning network named PolAlpha-Net (Polarimetric Alpha-guided Network), guided by polarization decomposition knowledge. This network utilizes the polarimetric decomposition parameter to learn stable features, enabling high precision lithology segmentation in vegetated areas. Our study utilized images from June, September and November, all of which were heavily vegetated, and performed segmentation tasks across four categories, including a background category, using existing geologic maps as ground truth. The results demonstrate that PolAlpha-Net obtains superior performance, achieving a mean Accuracy (mAcc) of 86.79%. Specifically, the accuracy for the four categories—background, Q4, Arjnd and γ2(1) 5 —were 77.32%, 94.19%, 85.93%, and 89.71%, respectively. These outcomes confirm that PolAlpha-Net is adept at learning stable features even under dense vegetation cover and possesses excellent generalization capabilities across different conditions. Consequently, lithologic mapping with PolAlpha-Net yielded results with impressive spatial continuity. The outcomes of this research confirm that integrating PolAlpha-Net with ALOS-1 dual-polarization SAR data produces high-quality lithological segmentation in vegetated areas, underscoring its practical ap plication value in remote sensing and geological analysis.

authors: Wanshi Chen, Lingjia Liu, Xiaofeng Liu, Erik G. Larsson, Mohamed El Jaafari, Tony Q. S. Quek, Robert Schober
