title: AISPO: Enhancing Depth Reliability for Robotic Manipulation of Non-Lambertian Objects via Affine-Invariant Shape Prior_supp1-3686704.mp4
authors: Zhiming Chen
publish: N/A
abstract: Reliable depth perception is critical for robotic manipulation, especially for non-Lambertian objects such as transparent or highly specular surfaces, where raw depth measurements are often corrupted or missing. These failures frequently propagate to motion planning, resulting in invalid grasp poses and execution errors. We propose AISPO, a depth completion framework that improves depth reliability for manipulation in challenging sensing conditions. AISPO combines multi-scale RGB-D feature fusion with an affine-invariant shape prior to enforce geometric consistency and mitigate catastrophic depth failures. Unlike methods that focus primarily on average depth accuracy, our approach emphasizes physical plausibility and structural integrity of the predicted depth maps. Extensive benchmark evaluations demonstrate competitive performance and strong generalization to unseen objects and novel scenes. Real-world grasping experiments further show that enhanced depth reliability significantly improves manipulation success rates, particularly for transparent objects where many existing methods fail to produce physically usable depth estimates.

title: Decoupled Heuristic Multi-Vehicle Emergency Trajectory Planning for Sudden Obstacles_supp1-3686646.mp4
authors: Huayan Pu
publish: N/A
abstract: The emergence of sudden obstacles can significantly reduce the feasible space and may induce locally non-convex or fragmented space, especially in densely clustered scenarios, making vehicle trajectory planning remarkably challenging. Current methods face computational bottlenecks when generating emergency trajectories under such tight real-time constraints. To address this issue, we decouple safety-critical guidance from trajectory optimization for suddenly appearing obstacles. Specifically, a novel unified nonpositivity quantification method based on vector cross-product consistency is introduced to numerically constrain non-convex regions and a heuristic risk metric is designed to guide the optimization of avoidance target. Additionally, a dynamic priority strategy is further designed to adaptively adjust the constraint dimensionality in real time, improving the success rate of emergency planning. Comparative evaluations with existing emergency planning methods demonstrate the superiority of the proposed approach in terms of success rate, planning time, and emergency trajectory length. Finally, several real-world multi-vehicle experiments validate the effectiveness and practical applicability of the proposed method.

title: Semantic Segmentation of Flooded Vegetation in SAR Imagery: A Benchmark Dataset and Comparative Study of Deep Learning Models
authors: Monika Gierszewska, Tomasz Berezowski
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Accurate flood extent mapping plays a critical role in disaster management and response. With the increasing availability of high-resolution and frequently acquired satellite imagery, Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) has become a valuable tool for flood delineation. However, one of the main challenges in SAR-based flood mapping remains the presence of vegetation, which obscures water surfaces and complicates detection. In this study, we introduce VegFlood, a reference dataset designed for training, validating, and testing deep learning models for semantic segmentation of flooded areas, including regions covered by vegetation. The dataset was constructed using publicly available Sentinel-1 SAR imagery, combined with high-resolution digital terrain models (DTMs) and hydrological observations from gauging stations. VegFlood comprises 1,707 image–mask pairs from 39 gauging stations, geographically limited to the territory of Poland. To establish baseline performance on the VegFlood test set, we conducted a series of experiments using state-of-the-art deep learning models for flood segmentation. The experimental results consistently demonstrate that FV is the most difficult class to detect, achieving substantially lower Intersection over Union (IoU), User Accuracy (UA), and Producer Accuracy (PA) values compared to OW and NF classes. Among the evaluated models, RS-Mamba achieved the highest performance, reaching a mean IoU of 0.566, with UA of 0.689 and PA of 0.689.Further analysis shows that segmentation accuracy is strongly influenced by vegetation density, flood water level, and local geomorphological conditions. These results confirm the limitations of C-band SAR imagery for flooded vegetation detection and highlight the importance of dedicated datasets that explicitly account for FV as a separate semantic class.

title: Is There A Bottom Line for Poisoning? Detecting High-Concealed Injection Attacks for Recommendation
authors: Yan Feng, Zhihai Yang, Kexin Li, Jianhua He, Jianxin Li, Pinghui Wang, Zhiquan Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: Recommender systems (RSs) are widely adopted due to their effectiveness in modeling user preferences and generating personalized recommendations. However, data poisoning attacks (PAs) manipulate recommendation results by injecting fake user profiles, thereby affecting the quality and accuracy of RSs. Moreover, emerging high-concealed PAs (HCPAs) achieve greater evasion of detection by controlling the cost of the attack, simulating the behavior patterns of benign users, and carrying out the attack with less prior knowledge. The HCPAs bring challenges: (1) the very low cost of attacks not only leads to an imbalance in data distribution but also introduces a large amount of accidental co-occurrence noise; (2) the behavioral patterns similar to benign users make it difficult to describe the characteristics of HCPAs; and (3) the prior knowledge for detecting HCPAs in real scenarios is very limited. To address these challenges, we propose STOP, an orthogonal projection bi-hypersphere detection method built on multi-view relational disentanglement and information-consistent fusion. First, we model the distributional preferences of user ratings to eliminate rating and popularity bias, and construct a co-occurrence association graph to suppress accidental overlaps. To address data imbalance caused by HCPAs, second, we introduce a distributional-consensus importance screening method that filters out benign users weakly associated with potential attackers. To address the issues of noise and the difficulty in feature characterization, third, we propose a multi-view relational disentanglement and information-consistent fusion method, which can eliminate redundant relationships, separate key relations into sequence-varying and sequence-stable components over rating sequences, and retain task-related relationships. Finally, inspired by the “convergence theorem”, we design an orthogonal projection bi-hypersphere boundary learning detection method to reduce the high false alarm rate (FAR). We extensively evaluate STOP under various HCPA scenarios, demonstrating its superiority over existing methods with an average 12.34% improvement in detection rate and an average 2.75% reduction in FAR. Furthermore, forensic analysis on real-world unlabeled data reveals distinct attacker “fingerprints”, such as extreme ratings, contradictory review styles, and analysis of target items, validating STOP's reliability in practical applications.

title: Theoretical and Experimental Research on Pirani Vacuum Sensors for In-situ Detection in Near-Space
authors: Dezhi Zheng, Jinghao Chen, Lijing Han, Minze Chen, Wenfeng Liu, Zhiao Zhu, Wenjing Zhang, Zhongxiang Li, Chun Hu, Weidong Dai
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: In this paper, a micro-electromechanical system (MEMS) ultra-low pressure sensor is developed, which is specifically tailored for rocket sounding systems and provides an in-situ detection approach for pressure measurement in the near-space environment. To enhance the overload resistance of the sensor, a composite film consisting of aluminum (Al) film and photosensitive polyimide (PSPI) film is adopted. Meanwhile, its sensitivity is improved by optimizing the structural grooves. The structural parameters of the sensor are determined through theoretical analysis and simulation modeling, and its overload resistance capability is evaluated via ground tests. The test results demonstrate that the sensor has a measurement range of 5 - 6000 Pa, an accuracy higher than 7.73%, and an overload resistance exceeding 200g. Furthermore, actual rocket sounding flight tests confirm that the sensor can effectively withstand the overload impact during rocket launch and successfully detect the air pressure data in the near-space range below 72 km, thus verifying its feasibility as an in-situ pressure detection method for near-space.

title: Tunable Spatial Power Splitter Using Single Layer Cylindrical Plasma Discharges
authors: Mohammad G. H. Alijani, Alessio Monti, Stefano Vellucci, Mirko Barbuto, Alessandro Toscano, Filiberto Bilotti
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: This work presents a novel single-layer, reconfigurable spatial power splitter based on plasma discharges. Each unit cell features two adjacent core–shell cylinders, composed of a tunable plasma core surrounded by a high-permittivity dielectric shell. The plasma core is modeled using a free-electron plasma permittivity with variable plasma frequency. Numerical simulation demonstrates that the power-splitting ratio can be continuously varied between 30% and 70% by independently controlling the plasma frequencies of the two cylinders. This reconfigurability enables precise manipulation of the transmitted power distribution while limiting the total reflected, absorbed, and undesired diffraction power to less than 10% of the incident energy, corresponding to an overall efficiency exceeding 90%.

title: Resilient Data-Driven Security Platoon Control for Heterogeneous Vehicle Systems Under Saturation and External Disturbances
authors: Xiaomiao Xie, Ying Wan, Liang Hua, Jinde Cao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: This paper proposed a model-free adaptive security control (MFASC) method designed for nonlinear vehicle systems, simultaneously tackling controller saturation, external disturbances, and aperiodic Denial-of-Service (DoS) attacks. To achieve a desired platoon control objective with only input-output data, the dynamics are reconstructed into a differential form with pseudo partial derivative parameters. Under saturation constraints, a de-saturation factor is introduced to ensure the controller’s state remains within permissible limits. Additionally, a disturbance observer is included to estimate external disturbances and mitigate their negative effects. For aperiodic DoS attacks, an attack compensation mechanism is developed. Comprehensive stability analysis demonstrates the ultimate boundedness of the formation error of vehicle systems under aperiodic DoS attacks. The validity of the theoretical results is supported by numerical simulations.

title: Double-RISs Aided Receive Polarization-Combined Space Shift Keying and Spatial Modulation Systems with SPTnet-Detector
authors: Yuyan Liu, Chaorong Zhang, Benjamin K. Ng, Chan-Tong Lam
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: Reconfigurable intelligent surfaces (RISs) have recently attracted significant attention for improving wireless performance, yet single-RIS deployments remain limited in terms of coverage and design flexibility. To further enhance transmission reliability and link quality, we propose double RISs receive polarization and spatial shift keying (D-RPSSK) and receive polarization and spatial modulation (D-RPSM) systems over Nakagami-m fading channels, introducing a new paradigm for next-generation wireless communications. By jointly exploiting polarization and spatial diversity, the proposed architecture improves spectral utilization and enhances link robustness. Building upon the system models, the novel theoretical average bit error probability (ABEP) expressions are derived using maximum likelihood (ML) detector. Moreover, a spatial and polarization Transformer-based (SPTnet) detector is developed to capture long-range dependencies, enabling accurate symbol recovery under complex propagation environments. This integration of D RISs-assisted index modulation and deep learning (DL)–based detector effectively overcomes challenges related to spectral efficiency (SE) and bit error rate (BER) under time-varying channels. Simulation results demonstrate the superiority of the proposed D-RISs schemes, showing significant improvements in both reliability and efficiency over conventional designs and show that the proposed SPTNet achieves near-ML detector accuracy and surpasses LSTM-based detector under complex channel conditions.

title: A Novel Active End-Effector Type Dynamic Arm Support System With Twisted String Actuator
authors: Sohyun Moon, Yeji Hwang, Suncheol Kwon, Jonghyun Kim
publish: IEEE/ASME Transactions on Mechatronics
abstract: Dynamic arm supports (DAS) help individuals with impaired upper limb function perform activities of daily living. However, most existing systems lack accurate gravity compensation based on user posture, and few studies have explored design methods to reduce required torque. In this study, we propose a novel end-effector-type active DAS (A-DAS) system using a spring joint mechanism driven by a deflected twisted string actuator. The design was optimized for maximum transmission ratio, compactness, and safety. We implemented an assistive force control strategy providing precise assistance based on the user's body weight and desired assistance level. To validate the system, we conducted a simulated eating task with 12 healthy participants. Results showed that the A-DAS provided superior control accuracy (0.48 versus 13.57 mm root-mean-squared error) compared to conventional passive systems. This led to significantly improved muscle activation patterns, showing both reduced activation range (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\kappa$</tex-math></inline-formula>) in the anterior deltoid (33.2% versus 60.9% reference voluntary contraction) and more stable activity (coefficient of variation) in the biceps (32.0% versus 49.0%). Future research will focus on expanding clinical applications to wider patient populations, including those with tremors.

title: A Study on the Flexible Scheduling Strategy for the Computing Power Load of Data Centers Based on Multi-algorithm Integration
authors: Yang Bai, Fan Wang, Hongliang Chu, Kang Huang, Qian Peng
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: Under the dual drive of the East-to-West Computing Resource Allocation and Dual Carbon strategies, the contradiction between the rigid growth of computing power load in data centers and the pressure of new energy consumption in the power grid has become increasingly prominent. This paper proposes and practices a complete theoretical and technical system for the collaborative optimization of computing and power, aiming to transform data centers from traditional rigid loads into flexible resources that support the construction of a new power system. This paper mainly makes the following three innovations: First, a closed-loop system engineering framework of monitoring dispatching evaluation has been constructed, achieving fullprocess management from panoramic monitoring of electricity and carbon, intelligent integrated dispatching to real-time feedback of effects. Secondly, a Power Computing Power Intraday Fusion Scheduling Optimization Model integrating multiple algorithms was proposed. This model adopts dual time scales of a minute level and a 15-minute level, aiming to minimize the total cost. It innovatively combines greedy algorithms, simulated annealing, and task exchange strategies to find an efficient solution, guiding the computing power load curve to actively follow the photovoltaic output curve. Thirdly, by integrating multimodal carbon data-driven and AI mechanismdriven hybrid modeling, a carbon emission knowledge graph and causal reasoning model have been constructed, enhancing the interdependence and strategy adaptation capabilities of carbon management. An empirical study taking the State Grid Jiangxi Electric Power Data Center as the object shows that this system can achieve cost savings in operation and effectively improve the utilization rate of clean energy. This research provides a replicable and scalable systematic solution for solving the problem of spatiotemporal mismatch of computer resources, which is of great theoretical and practical significance for promoting the green and low-carbon coordinated development of energy and digital infrastructure.

title: AI-Enhanced Intelligent Interfaces for Secure Biometric Authentication in IoT Ecosystems: Recent Advances and Future Directions
authors: Sofia Marilina Glorioso, Giuseppe Pappalardo, Salvatore Sorce, Vincenzo Conti
publish: 2026 International Conference on Semantic Computing (ICSC)
abstract: The ubiquitous integration of devices from the Internet of Things (IoT) into daily life demands authentication mechanisms that are secure, continuous, and unobtrusive. Traditional static credentials are increasingly inadequate for dynamic IoT environments. This work explores the paradigm shift towards AI-enhanced intelligent interfaces that leverage biometric authentication as an intrinsic component of user interaction. We analyse the convergence of three critical dimensions: (1) AI-driven intelligent interaction modalities, ranging from contactless RF sensing to wearable neuromuscular interpretation; (2) Advanced biometric authentication paradigms, focusing on behavioural traits and multimodal fusion strategies; and (3) Security and privacy constraints specific to IoT ecosystems, including edge intelligence and cancellable biometrics. By synthesising a selection of recent literature, this paper highlights how Artificial Intelligence is transforming interfaces from passive input points into active, security-aware agents capable of robust user identification in resource-constrained environments.

title: Electric Vehicle Charging Scheduling Optimization Model Based on Reinforcement Learning
authors: Peijun Li, Wen Wang, Xiangliang Fang, Ye Yang, Xiaoqing Zhang, Shun Li, Yi Long, Xiaorui Hu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: As electric vehicle (EV) becomes more widespread, the power grid is encountering significant issues such as load fluctuations and rising charging costs for users. To address these challenges, this paper introduces an optimization method for EV charging scheduling based on the Q-learning algorithm, which aims to lower both user charging costs and grid load fluctuations. Initially, a charging scheduling model is created with user charging costs and grid load standard deviation as the optimization targets, and the charging process is framed as a Markov decision process. Next, the Q-learning algorithm is employed to enhance the charging behavior of EVs, allowing for dynamic adjustments to charging times, avoiding peak grid loads, and achieving peak shaving and valley filling. Experimental results indicate that the proposed method can decrease the average user charging cost by 8.56% and the standard deviation of grid load by 9.4%. It also effectively balances load and manages costs even as the number of EVs increases, with particularly notable results during winter and summer. Compared to traditional methods, this approach offers benefits such as a shorter training period, faster convergence, and significant optimization results, providing an effective solution for EV charging scheduling.

title: Support Vector Machines: An Inclusive Survey in Financial Fraud Detection
authors: Ermir Balliu, Bilal Saoud, Ibraheem Shayea, Timur Grigoryev
publish: 2025 IEEE/ACIS 29th International Conference on Software Engineering, Artificial Intelligence, Networking and Parallel/Distributed Computing (SNPD)
abstract: In the financial industry, using of several Machine Learning (ML) algorithms and artificial intelligence (AI) models has simplified the solution and detection of main problems related to its wide landscape. One of the most broadly used techniques of ML is Support Vector Machine (SVM) which owns its specific algorithms appropriated to detect frauds in financial applications. The reason why it is widely used in this field is that it can work well with High Dimensional Data (HDD) in applications where the number of features is greater than number of observations. Also, this technique is less susceptible to overfitting of HDD which makes it suitable for use in these cases.

title: Research on Live Detection and Localization of Localized Insulation Defects in Distribution Cables Using FMCW
authors: Dewen Zhang, Ruihui Han, Xiaolong Xie, Jie Sheng, Zhongyu Wang, Chao Xu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: With the acceleration of urbanization, the large number of distribution cables has become a core component of modern distribution networks. Insulation defects such as localized aging, moisture, and damage to the outer sheath have become one of the main causes of power system failures, seriously affecting the safe operation of the grid. Frequency Modulated Continuous Wave (FMCW)-based method for locating localized cable defects offers higher sensitivity and detection range, showing broad application prospects in the cable field. However, this method is currently limited primarily to offline applications and cannot achieve online detection of cable defects. Building upon this method, this paper conducts research on the live detection and localization of partial insulation defects in power distribution cables. It discovers that the interval length of notch peaks can characterize the severity of defects and proposes the use of a single-cycle signal mixing processing method to avoid harmonic interference introduced by cycle superposition. This approach results in smoother spectrum curves and makes the notch positions more distinguishable.

title: Between Assistance and Dependence: University Students' Perceptions of Generative AI Tools
authors: Khaled Mohamad, Elif Mamedova
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: This study examines how university students engage with generative artificial intelligence (GenAI) tools, with particular attention to usage patterns, perceived usefulness, dependence, learning-related concerns, and ethical attitudes. Data were collected through an online survey of 275 undergraduate students from multiple faculties at a large university. The results reveal widespread adoption, with nearly 89% of students reporting use of GenAI several times per week or more, most commonly for homework and essay writing. Students rated GenAI as highly useful, while levels of dependence remained moderate. Regression analysis showed that both perceived usefulness and frequency of use were significant predictors of dependence, explaining approximately one-third of its variance. Although participants expressed moderate concerns about potential impacts on learning and academic integrity, they also recognized substantial educational benefits. Notably, a majority of students emphasized the need for clear institutional guidelines to support responsible use. Overall, the findings highlight the dual role of GenAI as both a powerful learning aid and a potential source of overreliance, providing timely empirical evidence to inform ethical integration and policy development in higher education.

title: Cross-Lingual Detection of Machine-Generated Text Using Multilingual Transformers and Balanced Data
authors: Batyr Sharimbayev, Symbat Duisen
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: The rise in the development of giant language models has also led to the need for a reliable approach in distinguishing human-generated text from AI-generated text, particularly across various languages. However, the majority of the current approaches to text detection have largely remained limited to the English language, thereby being ineffective in languages that have a different structure or limited resources. The current research proposes a remedy to this problem by developing and evaluating a cross-lingual text detection model based on multilingual transformer models. For the purpose of fine-tuning the models, the team utilized a balanced dataset consisting of 98,224 samples in nine different languages to determine whether the text was human-generated or AI-generated. Out of the models, the performance of the XLM-RoBERTa model was the best, achieving a result of 95.56% accuracy and 96.29% F1-score after the application of the threshold optimization technique. The model also demonstrated excellent performance in all the languages. However, the accuracy in the English language remained slightly lower because of the diversity in the writing style.

title: Fairness-Aware Recommendations for Kazakhstani Gaming Video Content
authors: Aibar Zhangabay, Medet Mukushev
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: This study develops a fairness-aware recommendation system for YouTube gaming content addressing underrepresentation of Kazakh-language videos and emerging creators. A post-processing re-ranking approach was applied to content-based and collaborative filtering baselines using multiplicative boost factors for Kazakh content (1.25x) and emerging creators (1.25x), with channel diversity penalties. Evaluation on 700 videos from seven channels demonstrated 75 % Kazakh representation and 85 % emerging creator representation in top-100 recommendations, exceeding fairness targets while maintaining 98 % engagement retention. Crossvalidation confirmed algorithm stability with coefficient of variation below 0.05 for primary metrics.

title: Human AI Collaboration Competence and Employee Productivity in Digital Workplaces: Evidence From the Mekong Delta, Vietnam
authors: Hong Minh Ngoc Tran
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: This study explores relationships among human AI collaboration competence and digital workplace conditions and digital productivity of employees in enterprises that have applied AI in Mekong Delta, Vietnam. A two stage design was applied in order to strengthen the measurement relevance and analytic rigor. In Stage 1, four experts who had at least five years of experience in the areas of HRM, AI application and HRM digital transformation were interviewed to refine the definition of constructs and improve the items for the context of work in the local environment. In stage 2, the study uses a convenience sampling method to collect survey data from a total of 146 employees in Can Tho, Hau Giang and Kien Giang based on the criteria of employees who use AI in daily work activities. Multiple linear regression was adopted to calculate the association of integration of the AI workflow, the quality of collaboration platforms, the skill of asynchronous collaboration, the digital overload, the human AI coordinations, and digital productivity of the employees. The results show that the model does a meaningful proportion of variance of the digital productivity of employees. Human AI coordination and integration of AI workflows are the variables that can be observed as the most positive relationship with productivity, but it can also be contributed by the quality of collaboration platforms as well as a skill of asynchronous collaboration. Digital overload is also negatively associated, which indicates that over digital demands can negate the benefit of productivity in technology mediated work. The findings are a reminder of how productivity in digital workplaces is not only about the capabilities to enable but also about how to manage overload as AI becomes part of everyday work flows.

title: Autoencoder-Based Resnet Block for Laser Phase Noise in Optical Communications
authors: Ahmed Al Asadi, Omar Alnaseri, Hussein Ali Attallah, Yassine Himeur, Mohammed Alaa Ala'anzy
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: This work investigates the use of ResNet-based AEs to reduce the impact of laser phase noise within coherent optical communication transmission. Traditional AE designs usually rely on dense layers, which are simple to implement but struggle with the complex structure of fiber-optic signals. To address this limitation, this work introduces a ResNet-based AE architecture for both the transmitter and receiver. The model is trained end-to-end to learn constellations and decoding strategies that are more robust to phase noise. Simulation results using 16-QAM show that the proposed ResNet-based design consistently outperforms dense-layer AEs in terms of AIR and BER, especially at larger laser linewidths. The results demonstrate that the residual structure enables deeper and more expressive feature learning, providing stronger resilience to laser phase noise compared to conventional dense architectures.

title: Leveraging Historical Performance Data to Improve Response Times in Multi-Channel E-Government Complaint Systems
authors: Evaristus Didik Madyatmadja, Vanessa Elizabeth Harianto, Daffa Farras Putra Tarigan
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: Digital complaint platforms have improved citizen access to government services; however, providing timely responses across different departments is challenging, especially when complaints are automatically closed after a period of inactivity. This study aims to create predictive models to identify complaints likely to be automatically closed within a multichannel e-government system, which would allow for proactive resource allocation and task management. Using a year's worth of operational data from an integrated platform, we developed features that represent departmental workload, historical performance metrics, and submission trends over time. Stratified cross-validation was employed to train and evaluate three machine learning models: Random Forest, XGBoost, and Logistic Regression. XGBoost proved to be the strongest performer, correctly flagging nearly two-thirds of atrisk complaints while accurately clearing 97.3% of cases that were properly handled. When examining what drove these predictions, SHAP analysis revealed that a department's own track record, specifically how quickly it resolved complaints within 30 days and how fast it responded within the first week, mattered far more than simply when a complaint was submitted. Timing of submission, it turned out, had almost no bearing on outcomes at all. What this tells us is that departments carry their history with them. Past performance isn't just a metric, it's an early warning signal. This opens the door for smarter, data-driven workload systems that anticipate bottlenecks before they spiral, shifting the focus from scrambling to catch up to getting ahead of demand in the first place.

title: An Intelligent Monitoring Framework for Risk Situations in Multi-Level Vulnerable Power Systems
authors: Ziyu Chen, Li Chao Li, Yaping Hu, Yingbi Sun, Qi Zou
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: The purpose of this study is to explore the intelligent monitoring and early warning system of power system risks, and to realize the visual identification of power system risk forms with the help of computer and communication technology, so as to better improve the risk monitoring level of power system. Therefore, the results show that the power system proposed in this paper can integrate and simplify the data, conduct risk assessment of the information of different terminal points, and the multi-level power system risk prevention method can realize the multi-level analysis of subsystems, terminal equipment, and sub-servers, and make intelligent risk predictions, with an average processing volume of 350 times, which is better than the standard power system, and the risk identification rate is 88.23%, which is better than the standard risk identification rate of 72.63%, and can carry out risk prevention in the subsystem to improve risk handling capabilities. Therefore, the multi-level power system risk monitoring method proposed in this paper can clearly analyze the logic of the system and optimize the risk transmission content to ensure the effectiveness of power detection and realize the intelligent risk management and control of the power system.

title: Multi-Objective Optimization Model for Grid Resource Allocation Considering Transmission and Distribution Tariff Mechanism
authors: Xiaoxue Liu, Dong Yao, Junjie Pan, Zheng Cao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: Based on a dual-objective model revised in line with the new Transmission and Distribution Tariff regulations, this study incorporates Transmission and Distribution Tariff-related constraints to better align the model with current regulatory requirements. The Pareto-optimal solution set generated by the NSGA-II algorithm effectively reflects the trade-offs among these three factors—the grid entity’s net present value (NPV), resource allocation weights, and regulatory compliance. By analyzing the allocation results of five candidate projects, the study explores the optimal configuration mode for grid resources under the influence of Transmission and Distribution Tariffs, thereby providing systematic insights for decision-making personnel. The results indicate that balanced resource allocation is essential under the new tariff rules. Grid entities should avoid concentrating resource allocation on a single project and should prioritize projects with short construction cycles and rapid returns.
